notes:

You can get mine from https://ghostbin.com/paste/7zyv8

This is set to very high settings on 1920x1080 edit as needed. It seems fullscreen is needed for the game to work properly via proton since windowed got my whole setup frozen.

Other than that and the ocassional stutter (reported on windows too) the game seems to run pretty well.

title: Outward

notes:
** TIP TO GET BETTER FPS: I've noticed this game has a bug which I've experienced in a few other games. If you turn graphics settings up, it works. But if you turn them back down again, it'll sorta work but your fps will suck. So what you have to do is turn them down lower than you actually want them, then reboot the game. Then turn them up to where you want them. That should help your fps issues ;) Made massive differences for me! **

Aside from that, the game works well without any tweaks needed. It just runs out of the box for me. I get crashes sometimes, but all my Windows games do this so it might just be my system. Performance is not as good as you'd expect considering the technical detail of this game, but I hear it's the same for Windows users anyway. I'm playing it split screen which definitely hurts performance more, but I use the High preset at 1080p and get around 48-60fps. Not too bad.
title: Outward

notes:
If people want the mouse spinning issue fixed then turn off mouse warp in lutris or go into winecfg and untick capture full screen mouse. 

This resolves that issue for me except inside crafting you can move mouse to second screen, but that is not a issue since normal playing it stays to primary. 

The issue I have atm is the action shortcut keys are not shown just empty circles, probably a fix around for that.
title: Outward

title: Outward

notes:
Updated Report:

The game runs reasonably well but needs mouse-warp/full-screen capture turned off or mouse movement issues occur after 20mins, you can do this easily using steamplayprefix scripts (gitlab) and running winecfg -> graphics tab. 
(note game still has minor mouse issues sometimes, this is a game-bug and not proton)

Second of all, if you try to run this game via Lutris (using the proton prefix etc..) you'll have missing fonts, such as those for quick access bar, so launch from steam!
title: Outward

notes:
Runs fine, however Borderless and Windowed modes rendered a black screen with just the menu. Changing to Fullscreen in the options made everything render correctly.
FPS is maybe 10 or so lower than on Windows and there's minor jitter sometimes.
My GPU temp ran hot (95C compared to 81C on Windows with the same settings), but I think that's more a problem with my system when playing via Wine (which is concerning).

title: Outward

notes:
Game work fine in full-screen. To solve the wonky mouse run this command without quotes: 

"WINEPREFIX=$HOME/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/794260/pfx winecfg"

 Make sure that "Automatically capture the mouse in full-screen windows" in the Graphics tab is not ticked and hit "Apply".

notes:
For BepinEx mod support set winhttp to "Native then Builtin" in wine config by
running: " WINEPREFIX=$HOME/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/794260/pfx winecfg " (no quotes) in console. Libraries tab, add winhttp using "new override for library box.

Reshade mods: Follow usual mod instructions and add (64bit) d3dcompiler_47.dll to game dictionary from here: https://www.reddit.com/r/linux_gaming/comments/b2hi3g/reshade_working_in_wine_43/
title: Outward

notes: Low performance, Only 30-50FPS even at Very Low to Medium settings, runs at 100-140 at High on Windows
title: Outward

notes:
Huzzah! After the latest proton update to 4.11-4, the mouse spazzing issue seems to have been solved.
Sadly, the performance is still not up to snuff. The framerate is quite unstable. So keep in mind you're going to need a fairly beefy machine. And then still won't be able to comfortably run this @ Ultra settings. I'm getting 30-50 fps @very high and 40-70 @high. Further down the settings gradient you hit diminishing returns, the potato setting barely pushes into the low 90s and only at moments, generally fluctuating between 50-90.
Note that some options, e.g., the render scale option, actually decrease performance at lower values, rather than increasing it, so you may want to tinker with individual options.
title: Outward
